subject: [PATCH v6 2/3] x86/insn-eval: Move assign_register() out of KVM as insn_assign_reg()

reviews.author: tip-bot2 (Meta)
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the comment in the switch statement for a 32-bit write incorrectly states that it zero-extends into the upper 32 bits, when in fact it should be zero-extending to 64 bits.

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch moves a helper function, assign_register(), from KVM's instruction emulator to a new header file <asm/insn-eval.h> as insn_assign_reg(). The function writes values into registers following x86 rules for general-purpose register writes. The same logic is needed by the TDX guest #VE handler for port I/O emulation and was previously duplicated in KVM. The patch adds no functional change but makes the code more maintainable and reusable.
